stem: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 70°. If triangle ABC is an isosceles triangle, which of the following could NOT be the measure of angle B?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The three interior angles of a triangle sum to 180°. With m∠A = 70°, if m∠B = 90°, then m∠C = 180 − 70 − 90 = 20°, so the angle measures would be 70°, 90°, and 20°. An isosceles triangle requires at least two angles of equal measure, and no two of these are equal. Choice A: 70°, 70°, 40° is isosceles. Choice B: 70°, 40°, 70° is isosceles. Choice D: 70°, 55°, 55° is isosceles. So 90° is the only value that does not yield an isosceles triangle.
